fit_reasons: yard area is well within the mower max (320 < 500 sqm)
slope is very gentle (4% < 30% max slope)
virtual RTK-GPS boundary is perfect for this fenced suburban lawn with open sky access

plan.boundary_placement: Virtual boundary mapped 15cm from the fence line using Navimow App. Set virtual exclusion zones around the flowerbed.
plan.dock_location: North-east corner of the lawn, near the outdoor GFCI outlet. Ensure clear view of the sky for GPS antenna.

fit_reasons: yard area is within mower capacity (3500 < 5000 sqm)
extremely steep slopes (65%) require Luba 2 AWD's industry leading 80% climbing ability
multi-zone GPS navigation allows wire-free setup across large, rough open terrain

plan.boundary_placement: RTK-GPS virtual boundary. Reference station mounted on roof of shed for optimal line-of-sight. Program exclusion zones for the rock piles and steep dropoffs.
plan.dock_location: Beside the garden shed. Plugged into the 230V outlet inside the shed via weatherproof cable path.
plan.schedule: Daily 10:00–14:00 (to maximize dry grass and solar charge conditions)

fit_reasons: yard area is within the mower max (1200 < 1600 sqm)
complex obstacles like pond and patio are handled expertly by Goat G1's 3D LiDAR and visual obstacle avoidance
virtual beacon technology is ideal for garden layouts that block standard GPS signals

plan.boundary_placement: Place 3 EcoVacs navigation beacons at perimeter corners for triangulation. Map virtual boundaries around the pond, playground, and patio area.
plan.dock_location: Patio edge, on flat concrete slab, near patio power outlet.
